name: Canvas2D Pseudo-3D Racing
description: Reusable, config-driven pseudo-3D road racer (OutRun style) using the classic projected-road-segment technique: a track of straight/curve/hill sections built into z-segments and projected each frame from a chase camera to fake 3D on a flat 2D canvas (no real 3D, no Three.js). Race the laps against the clock; grass off-road slows you; optional light traffic to weave through. Procedural fallback graphics; standalone browser export.
capabilities: classic pseudo-3D projected road segments (curves + hills)
chase-camera projection with configurable fov, camera height and draw distance
car physics: accelerate, brake, speed-scaled steering, centrifugal push on curves
off-road grass caps and bleeds speed
multi-lap racing with lap timer and best-lap tracking
optional simple traffic to weave through (rear-end slows you)
standalone export (relative paths, no server calls)
constraints: vanilla HTML/CSS/JS only — no Node, npm or frameworks
configuration-driven: generators produce JSON matching schema.json, never runtime code
relative asset paths only; missing sprites fall back to procedural drawings
pseudo-3D is a flat 2D projection — no real 3D, no Three.js; no branching routes in v1
